name: Sour Cream Pound Cake+ 2 Variations
recipeInstructions:
1. Let butter, eggs, and 1/2 cup of sour cream stand at room temperature for 30 minutes.
2. Grease and lightly flour an 8x4x2 or 9x5x3 inch loaf pan.
3. Stir together the flour, baking powder, and baking soda; set aside.
4. In a large mixing bowl, beat butter with an electric mixer on medium speed for 30 seconds.
5. Gradually add sugar, beating about 10 minutes or until light and fluffy.
6. Beat in vanilla.
7. Add eggs one at a time, beating for 1 minute after each addition.
8. Add the dry mixture and sour cream alternately to the beaten mixture, beating on low to medium speed after each addition just until combined.
9. Press batter into the prepared pan, spreading evenly.
10. Bake in an oven set to 325°F for 60 to 75 minutes or until a wooden toothpick comes out clean.
11. Cool in the pan on a wire rack for 10 minutes.
12. Remove cake from the pan; cool on a rack.
13. If desired, serve the cake with sour cream and fresh berries. Makes 10 servings.
Variations:
ORANGE-ROSEMARY POUND CAKE
Prepare as directed, except stir 1 1/4 teaspoons finely shredded orange peel and 1 teaspoon snipped fresh rosemary into the batter. If desired, garnish the cake with fresh rosemary.
LEMON-POPPY SEED POUND CAKE
Prepare as directed, except substitute 1/2 cup low-fat lemon yogurt for the sour cream. Add 2 tablespoons poppy seed, 1 teaspoon finely shredded lemon peel, and 2 tablespoons lemon juice to the batter.
